pattern: Run Dijkstra from node 0 and from node n-1; edge (a,b,w) is on a shortest path iff dist_from_0[a] + w + dist_from_n1[b] == shortest or dist_from_0[b] + w + dist_from_n1[a] == shortest.
hints: Compute single-source shortest distances from node 0 (dist0) and from node n-1 (dist_n1) using Dijkstra on the undirected graph.
The shortest path length from 0 to n-1 is dist0[n-1]. An edge (a,b,w) lies on at least one shortest path when dist0[a] + w + dist_n1[b] == shortest OR dist0[b] + w + dist_n1[a] == shortest.
If n-1 is unreachable from 0, no edges lie on a shortest path; return all false.

edge_inputs.description: two parallel paths, only the shorter one's edges qualify
edge not on any shortest path due to detour
multiple shortest paths sharing some edges
self-loop node (a==b) — should never be on path

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    edges = []
    # chain to ensure connectivity
    for i in range(n-1):
        edges.append([i, i+1, rng.randint(1, 100)])
    # extra random edges
    seen = set((e[0], e[1]) for e in edges)
    for _ in range(n // 2):
        a = rng.randint(0, n-1)
        b = rng.randint(0, n-1)
        if a != b and (a,b) not in seen and (b,a) not in seen:
            edges.append([a, b, rng.randint(1, 100)])
            seen.add((a,b))
    return [n, edges]
